title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen investigates the death of Andrew Van, a reclusive school principal in Arroyo who was known as a teetotaler and a model teacher. During an autopsy at the county courthouse, Coroner Stapleton reveals that Van's body has been stored for eight days and is headless. Ellery examines the victim's belongings, including a wallet, a watch, and a letter from a Pittsburgh bookstore, noting that several items bear the initials "AV."

During legal proceedings, farmer Michael O'Keefe testifies that he and "Old Pete" discovered Van's body nailed by the hands and feet to a signpost near Arroyo Peak. Mayor Matt Hollis clarifies that Old Pete is a local eccentric living in a small house near Arroyo. Mayor Hollis and Sheriff Rudd also testify regarding the discovery of the body and a T-shaped mark found on a door at the Fanns' residence.

Luther Bernheim, a local grocer, testifies that while Van was a regular customer who paid in cash, he was never truly friendly. Bernheim notes that Van had unusual tastes, specifically ordering expensive caviar, a detail that leads Ellery to suspect Van's past may have been more luxurious than his life in Arroyo suggested. Finally, District Attorney Crummett testifies that Van arrived in Arroyo nine years prior to take the teaching position, living on Arroyo Street with a servant named Klein.
